authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(2) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland streams, rivers, and canals)
sources.quotedText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions. From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish. In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Angora Creek not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Angora Creek not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50(b)(161) — Upper Truckee River and tributaries upstream from confluence with Showers Creek
sources.quotedText: (161) Upper Truckee River and tributaries upstream from confluence with Showers Creek (Alpine and El Dorado Cos.). Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(152) — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Tahoe Lake and tributaries)
sources.quotedText: (152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries (Placer and El Dorado Cos.). (A) Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout (B) Tahoe Lake within 300 feet of the mouth of its tributaries. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 256258, "Angora Creek", Stream, CA, El Dorado Co.
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":256258,"gaz_name":"Angora Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"El Dorado","objectid_1":67967}. Corrects prior file coordinates (-120.0424, 38.87695), which were off by ~1.9 km from GNIS's recorded mouth point (-120.020196, 38.874913) near the confluence with the Upper Truckee River close to Meyers; the file's prior value appears to have been guessed as a midpoint along the creek's course rather than pulled from GNIS (the creek's headwater point near Angora Lakes, also in GNIS's multipoint record, is at approximately -120.069180, 38.858894).

end.verbatim: Friday preceding the last Saturday in April

parameters.note: Angora Creek is not named in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it follows the general statewide stream regulation §5.85(a)(2): open all year, with a 5-trout harvest season from the last Saturday in April through Nov. 15 and a 0-trout, artificial-lures-with-barbless-hooks catch-and-release season from Nov. 16 through the Friday before the last Saturday in April. Although Angora Creek lies in the Lake Tahoe basin, it is NOT subject to a Tahoe special closure. It is a tributary of the Upper Truckee River — its mouth is at the Upper Truckee near Meyers, roughly 4–5 miles south of Lake Tahoe — so it is not a direct 'Tahoe Lake tributary' under §7.50(b)(152) (which the separate Upper Truckee listing at §7.50(b)(161) shows is treated apart from the Upper Truckee system). And §7.50(b)(161) reaches only the 'Upper Truckee River and tributaries upstream from confluence with Showers Creek'; Angora Creek joins the Upper Truckee well downstream (north) of the Showers Creek / Showers Lake confluence in the Meiss Country headwaters, so it falls outside the (161) special reach. This is an interpretive determination from the creek's course; verify against the current CDFW booklet.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year under the general statewide stream regulation: harvest season (last Saturday in April through Nov. 15, 5 trout) and a zero-trout catch-and-release season (Nov. 16 through the Friday before the last Saturday in April, artificial lures with barbless hooks only). Not subject to a Tahoe tributary special closure.
regulations.verbatimText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions. From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland streams during the open harvest season (last Saturday in April through November 15): five trout daily, ten in possession, with no gear restrictions.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit during the open harvest season (last Saturday in April through Nov. 15).
regulations.verbatimText: ...are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions.

regulations.humanSummary: Zero-trout (catch-and-release) from Nov. 16 through the Friday before the last Saturday in April; trout must be released unharmed.
regulations.verbatimText: From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies... In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

regulations.humanSummary: During the winter catch-and-release season (Nov. 16 through the Friday before the last Saturday in April) only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used, for all species; no gear restrictions during the open harvest season.
regulations.verbatimText: ...only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. Angora Creek is wholly within California (El Dorado Co.).

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+.
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
